jktekxksZaa ds [k.M vkSj LFkk;h iqy&lkoZtfud foRriksf"kr ifj;kstuk,a] fu;e] 1997 ds fu;e 3] 5] 11 ds lkFk ifBr jk"Vªh; jktekxZ vf/kfu;e] 1956 ¼1956 dk 48½ dh /kkjk 7 }kjk iznRr 'kfDr;ksa dk iz;ksx djrs gq,] vf/klwpuk la[;k dk-vk- 1123¼v½] rkjh[k 9 ebZ] 2008 dks ,rn~}kjk fo[kafMr djrh gS D;ksafd iz;ksDrk'kqYd laxzg.k O;ogk;Z ugha gksxk D;ksafd iz;ksDrk'kqYd laxzg.k ds ek/;e ls izkIr ekfld jktLo iz;ksDrk'kqYd laxzg.k ij gq, ekfld [kpZ ls de gksxkA [Qk- la- Hkkjkjkizk@13013@lhvks @2014&15@gxM+h fczt] vyds'k 'kekZ] la;qDr lfpo MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 21st October, 2014 S.O. 2739(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 7 of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956) read with Rule 3, 5, 11 of the National Highways Fee (Fee for the use of National Highways, Sections and Permanent Bridge— Public Funded Projects) Rules, 1997, the Central Government by notification number S.O. 1123(E), dated 9th May, 2008 of the Government of India had authorized Chief Engineer (National Highways), Karnataka, Public Works Department to collect fee on mechanical vehicles for the use of the Bridge across Hagari River at Paramadevanahalli at km 358.000 on Ankola-Gooty section of NH-63 in the State of Karnataka. Now, in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 7 of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956) read with Rules 3, 5, 11 of the National Highways Fee (Fee for the use of National Highways, Sections and Permanent Bridge—Public Funded Projects) Rules 1997, the Central Government hereby rescinds the notification number S.O. 1123(E), dated 9th May, 2008 as the collection of user fee would not be viable as the monthly revenue through user fee collection would be less than monthly expenditure for collection of user fee. [F. No. NHAI/ 13013/ CO/ 2014-15/Hagari Bridge] ALKESH SHARMA, Jt. Secy. -- 1 of 1 --
